title: Holomorphic Floer theory and the Fueter equation
speaker: Aleksander Doan
abstract: I will discuss an idea of constructing a category associated with a pair of holomorphic Lagrangians in a hyperkahler manifold, or, more generally, a manifold equipped with a triple of almost complex structures I,J,K satisfying the quaternionic relation IJ =-JI= K.  This category can be seen as an infinite-dimensional version of the Fukaya-Seidel category associated with a Lefschetz fibration.  While many analytic aspects of this proposal remain unexplored, I will argue that in the case of the cotangent bundle of a Lefschetz fibration, our construction recovers the Fukaya-Seidel ca

proof_stages.label: Classical Floer-Morse template
proof_stages.summary: The talk frames the problem as complexifying Floer theory and recalls the classical comparison in which Lagrangian Floer homology for the zero section and the graph of epsilon df in a cotangent bundle is identified, for small epsilon, with Morse homology via a bijection between holomorphic strips and gradient trajectories.

proof_stages.label: Finite-dimensional complex Morse theory via Fukaya-Seidel counts
proof_stages.summary: The speaker replaces a real Morse function by a holomorphic Morse/Lefschetz function and sketches a differential-equation model for the Fukaya-Seidel category whose objects are critical points, whose morphisms are phase-dependent gradient trajectories, and whose differentials and higher operations should count Floer-type maps from surfaces.

proof_stages.label: Holomorphic action functional on a hyperkähler path space
proof_stages.summary: The infinite-dimensional analogue is set up by taking a hyperkähler manifold with complex Lagrangians, forming the complex symplectic action functional on paths from one Lagrangian to the other, and identifying its critical points and gradient trajectories with intersection points and rotated holomorphic strips.

proof_stages.label: Fueter equation for operations
proof_stages.summary: The operations in the desired holomorphic Floer category are argued to be counts of maps from [0,1] times R^2 satisfying the Fueter equation, with Lagrangian boundary conditions, strip asymptotics in one noncompact direction, and intersection-point asymptotics in the other.

proof_stages.label: Analytic foundations for Fueter moduli spaces
proof_stages.summary: The speaker discusses the needed Fredholm and compactness theory, proposes a Floer-type energy given by the s-derivative, proves or states its topological expression via pairing with omega_I, and explains remaining issues such as perturbations, bubbling, and possible nonremovable singularities.

proof_stages.label: Hyperkähler convexity and maximum principle
proof_stages.summary: To control noncompact targets, the speaker defines IJK-convex functions and rho-cylindrical complex Lagrangians, then states a maximum principle ensuring that Fueter maps with fixed boundary/asymptotic data remain in a compact subset.

proof_stages.label: Complexified Floer cotangent theorem
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem asserts that for the complex cotangent bundle of an exact Lefschetz fibration, with Lagrangians the zero section and the graph of epsilon df, suitable hypercomplex structures give a bijection between Fueter maps and the finite-dimensional Floer planes defining the Morse-theoretic Fukaya-Seidel operations.

proof_stages.label: Categorical and external consequences
proof_stages.summary: The closing discussion proposes that the construction should fit into a two-category of complex Lagrangians in a hyperkähler manifold and should connect Fukaya-Seidel categories, four-dimensional Fueter equations, hypercontact/Weinstein geometry, Donaldson-Thomas theory, complex Chern-Simons theory, and categorified Atiyah-Floer phenomena.

examples.name: Cotangent bundles as IJK-convex domains
examples.note: An audience question prompts the speaker to explain that cotangent bundles, especially over Stein or almost complex convex bases, provide concrete instances of the hyperkähler convexity condition, with a plurisubharmonic base function plus fiber norm squared serving as the convex function.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker checks cotangent-bundle domains against the IJK-convexity notion, discussing the PSH plus fiber-norm-squared function and the Nijenhuis-tensor error in the calculation

examples.name: Complex Chern-Simons and categorified Atiyah-Floer picture
